Théodore Géricault

17 works in the gallery.
Click any image to enlarge. Use ← → or close with Esc.
Man Clutching a Horse in Water, after Poussin's "Deluge" (recto)
by Théodore Géricault
France, 19th century · c. 1816 · pen and brown ink and brush and brown wash over graphite
Man Clutching a Horse in Water, after Poussin's "Deluge" (recto); Compositional Study? (possibly for "Poussin's Deluge") (verso)
by Théodore Géricault
France, 19th century · c. 1816 · pen and brown ink and brush and brown wash over graphite
Compositional Study? (possibly for "Poussin’s Deluge") (verso)
by Théodore Géricault
France, 19th century · c. 1816 · brown wash and black crayon
Return from Russia
by Théodore Géricault
France, 19th century · c. 1818 · lithograph printed in black and ochre with watercolor added by hand
Fighting Horses
by Théodore Géricault
France, 19th century · c. 1820 · watercolor over graphite
The Raft of the Medusa
by Théodore Géricault
French Romanticism · 1818–1819 · Louvre, Paris
A real recent disaster: 147 men cut loose on a raft off Senegal after the captain took the lifeboats. Fifteen survived. Géricault visited morgues and built a model in his studio.
The Return from Russia
by Théodore Géricault
France · 1818 · Lithograph in black with tan tint stone on ivory wove paper

by Théodore Géricault
